serviceNotes.engine oil: Mercedes-Benz EU service specifications for W210 petrol models use fixed oil and filter change limits (typically 15,000 km or 12 months, whichever comes first) on the standard European maintenance plan.
serviceNotes.dust filter: Mercedes-Benz service literature refers to the cabin filter as a dust filter; replace per the maintenance display or fixed schedule (commonly every 2 years / 30,000 km).
serviceNotes.engine air filter: Engine air filter service follows Mercedes mileage-based inspection items on EU maintenance sheets (often 60,000 km or 48 months).
serviceNotes.brake fluid: Replace brake fluid every 24 months per Mercedes-Benz time-based requirement; do not use a km value.
serviceNotes.check brake pad thickness: Pad thickness checks are part of each inspection service; 30,000 km aligns with biennial oil-service cadence when oil is at 15,000 km.
serviceNotes.spark plugs: Spark plug renewal for M112/M113-era petrol engines is specified at extended mileage and time limits (commonly 60,000 km or 48 months on EU sheets—verify for your exact engine code).
serviceNotes.coolant: Coolant renewal interval is specified in the vehicle maintenance booklet (many W210 petrol variants list long-life coolant exchange around 160,000 km).
serviceNotes.automatic transmission fluid: Automatic transmission fluid is officially long-life on many W210 gearboxes; fluid service around 60,000 km is a widely recommended preventive interval.
serviceNotes.manual transmission oil: Manual gearbox oil is typically long-life; renewal near 100,000 km is a common independent recommendation.
serviceNotes.transfer case oil: 4MATIC models only. Mercedes often documents transfer case fluid as inspection-only or long-life; many specialists suggest fluid renewal around 60,000 km on permanent AWD variants.
serviceNotes.front axle differential oil: 4MATIC models only. Front differential fluid complements transfer case service on AWD W210 models when not specified as lifetime.
serviceNotes.rear axle differential oil: Applies to all models; on 4MATIC vehicles coordinate with other axle fluids. Independent specialists often suggest 60,000 km when OEM lists no interval.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: Supplementary coolant exchange restores inhibitor package ahead of the OEM long interval if the vehicle sees severe use.
serviceNotes.poly-V belt: Accessory belt and tensioner replacement around 100,000 km reduces risk of sudden loss of charging and power steering assist.
